optimizations.name: BF16 Model Cast -- Enable Tensor Cores on All GEMMs

bottleneck.description: All 792 GEMM kernel launches dispatch FP32 SIMT path (ampere_sgemm_* family). The A100 Tensor Core units (HMMA) are completely inactive: smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 0 across all mm/addmm kernels. FP32 on A100 delivers 19.5 TFLOPS peak; BF16 Tensor Cores deliver 312 TFLOPS -- a 16x higher peak throughput. Together these two operators account for 67.55% of total profiled wall time.

aten::addmm_0.note: 0.193% tensor_core_active is rounding noise -- SIMT path confirmed by ampere_sgemm kernel name (no xmma substring)

transformation.description: Cast model weights and input tensors to torch.bfloat16. This forces cuBLAS to select the HMMA Tensor Core dispatch path (sm80_xmma_gemm_bf16bf16_bf16bf16_f32) instead of the SIMT sgemm path. BF16 is preferred over FP16 for GPT-2 because BF16 has the same dynamic range as FP32 (8 exponent bits), making it numerically safe for the large activation magnitudes in transformer FFN layers without requiring loss scaling.
fx_steps: # Step 1: Cast model parameters and buffers to BF16
# Step 2: For GPT-2, the primary input is input_ids (int64) -- no cast needed.
# If passing explicit float attention_mask, cast it too:
# Step 3: Compile AFTER dtype change so Inductor traces the BF16 weight graph
transformation.code_hint: model.to(torch.bfloat16) must precede torch.compile(). Dynamo traces the dtype-cast graph; casting after compile() leaves FP32 weight nodes in the traced graph.
transformation.contraindication_check: GPT-2 uses tied embeddings (wte.weight shared with lm_head). The embedding lookup uses integer gather (dtype-agnostic on the weight), so BF16 cast is safe. No contraindication applies.

estimated_impact.realistic_speedup_on_affected_ops: 4x-8x (GEMMs are compute-bound at 72-74% SM throughput in FP32, so most of the Tensor Core throughput uplift is capturable)
estimated_impact.latency_reduction_estimate_ns: approximately 50000000-60000000 ns saved from GEMM time alone

optimizations.confidence_justification: cuBLAS kernel selection based on dtype is deterministic and documented. BF16 input to mm on Ampere always routes to the xmma path. The 0.0% tensor_core_active counter is definitive proof of the FP32 SIMT path. No graph-level pass required.

risks.description: BF16 has 7 mantissa bits vs FP32's 23. For GPT-2 inference this is well-studied and accepted -- HuggingFace serves BF16 GPT-2 in production. Perplexity increase on WikiText-103 is typically < 0.1 PPL.
risks.mitigation: Run eval on downstream task before deploying. If regression exceeds tolerance, use torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = True as a gentler alternative (TF32 keeps FP32 storage, reduces mantissa to 10 bits).

risks.description: GPT-2 shares wte.weight between input embedding and lm_head. Casting to BF16 affects both paths. Embedding gather at BF16 is safe. lm_head logit precision is slightly reduced but acceptable for generation.
risks.mitigation: None required for inference. For perplexity-sensitive benchmarks, isolate lm_head: model.lm_head.weight = model.lm_head.weight.float() -- note this breaks weight tying and doubles memory for that tensor.

optimizations.name: torch.compile with fullgraph=True and mode='max-autotune'

bottleneck.description: Current compile mode is default inductor. After BF16 cast, GEMMs will reach Tensor Cores but tile sizes remain at heuristic defaults. max-autotune runs the Triton autotuner over the full GEMM tile space and selects empirically optimal configurations. fullgraph=True prevents graph breaks that cause eager-mode subgraph fallbacks, allowing Inductor to see the full computation graph for cross-operator fusion decisions.

evidence.note: waves_per_sm=3.556 indicates adequate wave-level parallelism; autotuning benefit comes from tile shape optimization (larger M/N/K tiles better saturate L2 and SMEM pipelines after Tensor Core activation)

transformation.description: Switch torch.compile arguments to enable full-graph tracing and autotuning. No FX pass required.
fx_steps: # Apply AFTER OPT-1 (model must already be BF16 before compiling)
#   1. Triton GEMM autotuning: tries tile configs (128x256, 64x256, 256x128, ...)
#      and picks the empirically fastest for each unique GEMM shape
#   2. Epilogue fusion: fuses bias-add and activation into the GEMM kernel exit
#   3. Persistent kernel selection for small GEMMs
# Cache autotuning results to avoid re-tuning on subsequent runs:
transformation.code_hint: First compile pass with max-autotune is slow (autotuning runs ~100 tile configs per unique GEMM shape). GPT-2 small has approximately 4 unique GEMM shapes; expect 60-180 seconds on first compilation.

estimated_impact.description: After BF16 Tensor Cores are active, max-autotune typically delivers 15-40% additional throughput on transformer GEMMs by selecting larger tiles (e.g. 128x256 vs default 64x64) that better saturate L2 and SMEM pipelines.

optimizations.confidence_justification: torch.compile mode='max-autotune' is a first-class supported feature. GPT-2 with HuggingFace modeling code has no documented graph-break issues with fullgraph=True. SM throughput of 72-74% in FP32 SIMT will improve under autotuned BF16 tiles.

risks.description: max-autotune significantly increases first-compile time. For GPT-2 small, expect 1-3 minutes of autotuning on first run.
risks.mitigation: Enable fx_graph_cache (shown above). Compiled artifacts are cached to disk and reused on subsequent runs with identical input shapes.

risks.description: fullgraph=True errors on graph breaks caused by dynamic shapes or Python control flow that depends on tensor values.
risks.mitigation: Fix input shapes for benchmarking, or use dynamic=True: torch.compile(model, mode='max-autotune', fullgraph=True, dynamic=True).

optimizations.name: BF16 SDPA Dispatch -- Eliminate Register Spills and Occupancy Collapse on Attention

bottleneck.description: The FP32 cutlass efficient-attention kernel (fmha_cutlassF_f32_aligned_64x64_rf_sm80) uses 168 registers per thread. Ampere SMs have 65536 registers; at 168 regs/thread a warp of 32 threads occupies 5376 registers, limiting the SM to ~12 active warps out of a theoretical maximum of 64. This collapses achieved occupancy to 6.24% and forces 24.47 MB of register state to spill to local memory (DRAM). After BF16 cast (OPT-1), PyTorch dispatches to the FlashAttention-2 BF16 kernel which uses approximately 64 registers per thread -- a 2.6x reduction that raises occupancy to ~30-40% and eliminates spills entirely.

transformation.description: OPT-1's BF16 model cast is sufficient to trigger this improvement -- no separate FX pass is needed. When model weights and activations are BF16, F.scaled_dot_product_attention dispatches to the BF16-optimized FlashAttention path automatically. The is_causal=True argument should be set explicitly to enable the fused causal mask path and avoid materializing an O(seq^2) mask tensor.
transformation.location: GPT-2 attention module -- verify SDPA call site uses is_causal=True
fx_steps: # Primary fix: OPT-1 (BF16 cast) triggers BF16 FlashAttention dispatch automatically.
# Ensure the SDPA call uses is_causal=True for decoder-only causal attention:
query,       # shape: [batch, heads, seq, head_dim], dtype=bfloat16 after OPT-1
key,         # shape: [batch, heads, seq, head_dim], dtype=bfloat16 after OPT-1
value,       # shape: [batch, heads, seq, head_dim], dtype=bfloat16 after OPT-1
is_causal=True,   # fused causal mask -- critical for decoder-only models
# With BF16 inputs on A100 (sm_major=8), PyTorch selects flash_fwd_kernel
# Confirm dispatch: torch.backends.cuda.flash_sdp_enabled() should return True.
transformation.code_hint: The HuggingFace GPT-2 implementation uses F.scaled_dot_product_attention in transformers >= 4.36. Check: grep -n 'scaled_dot_product_attention' $(python -c 'import transformers; print(transformers.__file__.replace("__init__.py",""))models/gpt2/modeling_gpt2.py')

estimated_impact.expected_end_to_end_speedup: 1.05x-1.1x incremental (attention is 3.18% of current time; proportional share grows after OPT-1 reduces GEMM dominance)
estimated_impact.dram_traffic_reduction_pct: ~60% (FlashAttention fuses QK^T softmax V into single SRAM-resident tiled kernel)

optimizations.confidence_justification: The register pressure root cause is directly readable from the profile: 168 regs/thread is a documented characteristic of fmha_cutlassF_f32 on Ampere. PyTorch SDPA dispatcher selects FlashAttention for BF16/FP16 inputs on sm80+ -- this is tested behavior in PyTorch >= 2.0. The 24.47 MB local memory spill evidence uniquely fingerprints this kernel variant.

risks.description: FlashAttention BF16 uses tiled softmax with online normalization, a different accumulation schedule vs the unfused FP32 path. Output logits may differ at the 1e-2 level from FP32 reference.
risks.mitigation: Run reference comparison: max absolute difference between FP32-unfused and BF16-Flash outputs is typically < 0.01 for standard sequence lengths.

risks.description: FlashAttention dispatch for BF16 requires torch >= 2.0 and sm_major >= 8. A100 qualifies on both counts.

optimizations.name: QKV Projection Fusion -- Eliminate Wave Overhead on Small Attention Projection GEMMs
operators: aten::mm nodes with shape [512, 768] x [768, 768] (Q, K, V projections, 36 GEMMs across 12 layers -- ranks 149+ in triage)

bottleneck.class: wave_starvation (secondary, on small attention projection GEMMs)
bottleneck.description: The attention Q, K, V projections have shape [512, 768] x [768, 768] and achieve waves_per_sm=1.778 -- below the 2-wave threshold where scheduling overhead becomes non-trivial. Fusing Q+K+V into a single [512, 768] x [768, 2304] GEMM triples the N dimension, increases waves_per_sm to approximately 5.33, and reduces kernel launches from 36 to 12 across the 12-layer model. After BF16 cast, the wider matrix also enables better Tensor Core tile utilization (larger tile configs fit the N=2304 dimension more efficiently than N=768).

evidence.collective_time_note: 36 such GEMMs appear in triage ranks 149+; individually below 5% threshold but collectively approximately 2% of total time

transformation.description: FX graph pass that detects three aten.mm nodes sharing the same input activation, concatenates their weight matrices into a single fused weight [768, 2304], and replaces the three mm calls with one mm followed by chunk(3, dim=-1). Must be applied after BF16 cast so the fused weight buffer is BF16.
transformation.location: Custom FX pass, or verify existing fusion in HuggingFace GPT-2 c_attn layer
fx_steps: Fuse 3 mm(x, W_q), mm(x, W_k), mm(x, W_v) with identical input x
into mm(x, W_qkv) followed by chunk(3, dim=-1).
Prerequisite: model must already be cast to BF16 (OPT-1 applied).
# Extract weight tensors, looking through aten.t() wrappers
# Only fuse if all three weights share the same shape [K, N]
if len(set(w.shape for w in weights)) != 1:
transformation.code_hint: HuggingFace GPT2Attention uses self.c_attn = Conv1D(3 * n_embd, n_embd), which projects to 3*768=2304 in a single call. If your model uses this standard implementation the QKV fusion already exists at the module level and this pass will find zero matching 3-node groups -- it is safe to run (no-op) but unnecessary. Verify: model.transformer.h[0].attn.c_attn.weight.shape should be (768, 2304) or (2304, 768).

estimated_impact.note: Lower priority because attention projection GEMMs are a small fraction of total time. Impact grows proportionally as GEMM time drops after OPT-1.

optimizations.confidence_justification: HuggingFace GPT-2 already implements QKV fusion via c_attn Conv1D in standard builds -- this pass may be a no-op. Confidence is medium because the actual Inductor-compiled graph must be inspected to confirm whether a fused or split weight representation was materialized. The waves_per_sm=1.778 evidence at triage ranks 149+ is real but represents a small time fraction.

risks.description: If HuggingFace GPT-2 already performs QKV fusion via c_attn, the pass finds zero matching 3-mm groups and exits cleanly.
risks.mitigation: Always call gm.graph.lint() after the pass. The implementation is designed to be a safe no-op when the pattern is absent.

risks.description: Concatenating Q+K+V creates a new 9 MB buffer (3 * 768 * 768 * 2 bytes BF16) while originals may persist briefly. Negligible on an 80 GB A100.

optimizations.name: TF32 Global Flag -- Fallback Tensor Core Activation for Any Residual FP32 GEMMs
operators: Any aten::mm or aten::addmm that cannot be cast to BF16 (loss computation, any FP32 subgraph)

bottleneck.class: tensor_core_idle (residual, applies if OPT-1 is partially applied or for FP32 subgraphs)
bottleneck.description: TF32 uses FP32 storage but routes GEMMs through Tensor Core hardware with 10-bit mantissa precision. It delivers 156 TFLOPS on A100 -- 8x faster than FP32 SIMT -- with no dtype cast required. This is a defensive one-line flag to ensure any FP32 GEMM remnants that survive OPT-1 still benefit from Tensor Cores.

evidence.note: These will be fully addressed by OPT-1. OPT-5 provides defense-in-depth for any residual FP32 paths.

transformation.description: Set the global TF32 matmul flag before model initialization. Zero graph modification required.
transformation.location: Script initialization, before model construction and torch.compile()
fx_steps: # Enable TF32 for matmul (routes FP32 mm through Tensor Cores, 10-bit mantissa)
# Enable TF32 for cuDNN (not applicable to GPT-2 but harmless)
# Note: allow_tf32 = True is the default in PyTorch >= 1.12.
# Verify it has not been disabled elsewhere in the codebase.

estimated_impact.expected_speedup_if_OPT1_not_applied: 4x-6x on GEMM-bound operators (FP32 SIMT -> TF32 Tensor Core path)
estimated_impact.expected_incremental_speedup_after_OPT1: 1.0x-1.05x (minimal; OPT-1 already routes to BF16 Tensor Core path which is faster than TF32)
estimated_impact.note: Primary value is as a safety net for residual FP32 subgraphs.

optimizations.confidence_justification: allow_tf32 is a documented, stable PyTorch flag. On Ampere hardware it deterministically routes FP32 GEMMs through Tensor Core hardware with TF32 precision. No graph-level change required.

risks.description: TF32 reduces mantissa from 23 bits to 10 bits. Relative error is at most 1e-3 per operation. Transformer inference is empirically insensitive to this change.
risks.mitigation: Disable with torch.backends.cuda.matmul.allow_tf32 = False if exact FP32 reproduction is required (e.g., for deterministic unit test baselines).

edges.reason: torch.compile must see BF16 weights at trace time to lower mm nodes to BF16 Tensor Core kernels in Inductor
SDPA FlashAttention dispatch requires BF16/FP16 Q/K/V inputs; OPT-1 provides this via the model-level dtype cast
Fused QKV weight buffer must be BF16 to match the runtime dtype of mm inputs after OPT-1; fusing at FP32 then casting later causes dtype mismatch errors

application_order: OPT-5 (global flag, any time before model construction)
OPT-4 (QKV FX pass, if needed -- run before or instead of torch.compile)
OPT-2 (torch.compile with max-autotune, wraps the BF16 model)
OPT-3 (cascades automatically from OPT-1 via SDPA dtype dispatch)

global_notes: All ncu replay duration values are 2-5x longer than actual wall-clock execution due to profiling serialization overhead. Use values for relative comparison only, not as absolute latency targets.

A100 SXM4-80GB ridge point for BF16: 156 FLOP/byte. The two dominant GEMM shapes are [512, 3072] x [3072, 768] (arithmetic intensity ~341 FLOP/byte) and [512, 768] x [768, 768] (arithmetic intensity ~256 FLOP/byte). Both are above the ridge point -- these GEMMs are compute-bound in BF16, confirming that full Tensor Core utilization after OPT-1 is achievable without hitting HBM bandwidth limits.

The dominant Triton fused kernel 'triton_poi_fused_add_addmm_mul_pow_tanh_view_5' (layer::duplicate::modules_* operators) achieves 76% occupancy and is already well-optimized by Inductor. This kernel represents GPT-2's GELU (tanh approximation) fused with LayerNorm and residual add. No further optimization is warranted on this kernel family.

12.6% of kernels (240 launches) are unattributed in the profile. These are Triton-compiled fused operators that escaped NVTX attribution. Their contribution is implicitly present but not individually visible. This does not affect the correctness of the top-3 bottleneck identifications since the attributed operators alone account for 70%+ of profiled time.
GPT-2 uses tied embeddings (wte.weight == lm_head.weight). BF16 cast is safe for both paths; see OPT-1 risks for details.

Expected combined speedup applying OPT-1 + OPT-2 + OPT-3 together: 3x-6x end-to-end wall-clock time reduction. Lower bound assumes conservative tile efficiency; upper bound assumes full Tensor Core saturation at autotuned optimal tile sizes.
